methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Locus Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Augury is a Series F robotics and manufacturing company founded by Gal Shaul and Saar Yoskovitz that has raised $75M to date. The valuation universe comprises 9 private peers (including Locus Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable, offering directional context for frontier-stage robotics valuations.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($75M) as a proxy base and applying a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement dynamics yields $98M. This method directly reflects the company's progression from $75M raised through Series F. Peer-median triangulation could not be applied; while 11 robotics and manufacturing peers exist broadly, only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer met narrow filtering criteria—below the 3-datapoint minimum for reliable median calculation. Reverse-engineering from public comparables or revenue multiples was not feasible given missing revenue and profitability data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $83M (low) to $112M (high), centered on $98M. This range reflects typical Series F step-ups applied to capital-raised proxies. Primary uncertainties stem from the single-method reliance; a more robust triangulation would require either closer peer revenue/multiple visibility or Augury's own financial metrics. The $83M–$112M band should be stress-tested against any disclosed recurring revenue or customer concentration data as it becomes available.
